description: Papers on offline/batch reinforcement learning applied to healthcare and clinical decision-making, 2020-2026.

papers.label: Model Selection for Offline Reinforcement Learning: Practical Considerations for Healthcare Settings
papers.authors: Shengpu Tang, Jenna Wiens
papers.detail: Investigates model selection for offline RL in healthcare using OPE as a proxy for validation. Finds FQE consistently leads to best validation ranking. Proposes a two-stage approach to accelerate model selection.

papers.label: Offline Reinforcement Learning with Uncertainty for Treatment Strategies in Sepsis
papers.authors: Ran Liu, Joseph L. Greenstein, James C. Fackler, Jules Bergmann, Melania M. Bembea, Raimond L. Winslow
papers.detail: Novel application of RL for sepsis treatment recommendations with confidence estimation. Identifies RL bias against aggressive intervention due to confounding. Mitigates bias using subspace learning.

papers.label: Proximal Reinforcement Learning: Efficient Off-Policy Evaluation in Partially Observed Markov Decision Processes
papers.authors: Andrew Bennett, Nathan Kallus
papers.detail: Extends proximal causal inference to POMDPs for off-policy evaluation. Introduces bridge functions for identification. Demonstrates on sepsis management.

papers.label: Multi-Objective SPIBB: Seldonian Offline Policy Improvement with Safety Constraints in Finite MDPs
papers.authors: Harsh Satija, Philip S. Thomas, Joelle Pineau, Romain Laroche
papers.detail: Safe Policy Improvement under constraints with multiple rewards. Provides high-probability guarantees. Evaluated on IV fluids and vasopressors for sepsis treatment.

papers.label: Semi-Markov Offline Reinforcement Learning for Healthcare
papers.authors: Mehdi Fatemi, Mary Wu, Jeremy Petch, Walter Nelson, Stuart J. Connolly, Alexander Benz, Anthony Carnicelli, Marzyeh Ghassemi
papers.detail: Proposes Semi-MDP framework for offline RL with variable-time actions. Introduces SDQN, SDDQN, SBCQ algorithms. Demonstrates MDP methods fail on variable-time environments. Validated on warfarin dosing.

papers.label: Towards Safe Mechanical Ventilation Treatment Using Deep Offline Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Flemming Kondrup, Thomas Jiralerspong, Elaine Lau, Nathan de Lara, Jacob Shkrob, My Duc Tran, Doina Precup, Sumana Basu
papers.detail: DeepVent: CQL-based agent for ventilator parameter optimization. Clinically relevant intermediate reward addresses sparse rewards. Recommends parameters within safe ranges per clinical trials.

papers.label: Offline Reinforcement Learning for Safer Blood Glucose Control in People with Type 1 Diabetes
papers.authors: Harry Emerson, Matthew Guy, Ryan McConville
papers.detail: Evaluates BCQ, CQL and TD3-BC for insulin dosing. Trained on less than a tenth of online RL samples. Increases time in healthy range from 61.6% to 65.3% without increasing hypoglycemia.

papers.label: Deep Offline Reinforcement Learning for Real-world Treatment Optimization Applications
papers.authors: Milashini Nambiar, Supriyo Ghosh, Priscilla Ong, Yu En Chan, Yong Mong Bee, Pavitra Krishnaswamy
papers.detail: Introduces transition sampling to address action imbalance in offline RL. Compares DDQN vs CQL on diabetes and sepsis tasks. Shows substantial improvements over standard CQL.

papers.label: Delphic Offline Reinforcement Learning under Nonidentifiable Hidden Confounding
papers.authors: Alizée Pace, Hugo Yèche, Bernhard Schölkopf, Gunnar Rätsch, Guy Tennenholtz
papers.detail: Tackles hidden confounding in nonidentifiable offline RL settings. Proposes delphic uncertainty from world model variation. Constructs pessimistic offline RL algorithm. Validated on sepsis benchmark and EHR.

papers.label: Towards Real-World Applications of Personalized Anesthesia Using Policy Constraint Q Learning for Propofol Infusion Control
papers.authors: Xiuding Cai, Jiao Chen, Yaoyao Zhu, Beimin Wang, Yu Yao
papers.detail: Proposes Policy Constraint Q-Learning (PCQL) for anesthesia. Adds policy constraint to CQL to keep agent distribution close to anesthesiologist. SHAP interpretability analysis.

papers.label: Reinforcement Learning in Dynamic Treatment Regimes Needs Critical Reexamination
papers.authors: Zhiyao Luo, Yangchen Pan, Peter Watkinson, Tingting Zhu
papers.detail: Critical examination of offline RL in DTRs. With 17,000+ experiments on sepsis data, shows RL performance varies significantly with evaluation metrics and MDP formulations. RL can be surpassed by random baselines.

papers.label: Empowering Clinicians with Medical Decision Transformers: A Framework for Sepsis Treatment
papers.authors: Aamer Abdul Rahman, Pranav Agarwal, Rita Noumeir, Philippe Jouvet, Vincent Michalski, Samira Ebrahimi Kahou
papers.detail: Medical Decision Transformer (MeDT) based on goal-conditioned RL. Uses acuity scores for conditioning. Enables clinician-model interaction. Competitive with CQL, BCQ while more interpretable.

papers.label: Offline Guarded Safe Reinforcement Learning for Medical Treatment Optimization Strategies
papers.authors: Runze Yan, Xun Shen, Akifumi Wachi, Sebastien Gros, Anni Zhao, Xiao Hu
papers.detail: Proposes OGSRL with dual constraint mechanism: OOD guardian for clinically validated regions and safety cost constraint encoding physiological boundaries. Theoretical guarantees on safety and near-optimality.

papers.label: Hybrid Adaptive Conformal Offline Reinforcement Learning for Fair Population Health Management
papers.authors: Sanjay Basu, Sadiq Y. Patel, Parth Sheth, Bhairavi Muralidharan, Namrata Elamaran, Aakriti Kinra, Rajaie Batniji
papers.detail: HACO framework: separates risk calibration from preference optimization. Uses conformal threshold for safe action masking. Validated on 2.77M decisions across 168K patients. AUC ~0.81 for risk discrimination.

papers.label: From Robotics to Sepsis Treatment: Offline RL via Geometric Pessimism
papers.authors: Sarthak Wanjari
papers.detail: Geo-IQL: augments IQL with kNN-based density penalty for OOD conservatism. On MIMIC-III sepsis, standard IQL collapses to BC (75% agreement) while Geo-IQL achieves 86.4% terminal agreement.

papers.label: Ensuring Safety in Automated Mechanical Ventilation through Offline Reinforcement Learning and Digital Twin Verification
papers.authors: Hang Yu, Huidong Liu, Qingchen Zhang, William Joy, Kateryna Nikulina, Andreas A. Schuppert, Sina Saffaran, Declan Bates
papers.detail: Transformer-based CQL (T-CQL) with uncertainty quantification for mechanical ventilation. Digital twin for online bedside evaluation. Outperforms state-of-the-art offline RL methods.

papers.label: Insulin4RL: Real-Time Insulin Management in the Intensive Care Unit for Offline Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Thomas Frost, Steve Harris
papers.detail: Insulin4RL dataset: naturally irregular inputs and actions from MIMIC-IV. 375K+ decisions across 12,209 patients for insulin infusion titration. Baseline metrics using model-free offline RL.

papers.label: Synthetic Acute Hypotension and Sepsis Datasets Based on MIMIC-III and Published as Part of the Health Gym Project
papers.authors: Nicholas I-Hsien Kuo, Mark Polizzotto, Simon Finfer, Louisa Jorm, Sebastiano Barbieri
papers.detail: Synthetic datasets for acute hypotension and sepsis using GANs and MIMIC-III. Very low identity disclosure risk (0.045%). Published as part of the Health Gym project for offline RL development.

papers.label: Offline Policy Evaluation and Optimization under Confounding
papers.authors: Chinmaya Kausik, Yangyi Lu, Kevin Tan, Maggie Makar, Yixin Wang, Ambuj Tewari
papers.detail: Maps landscape of offline policy evaluation under confounding. Characterizes settings where consistent estimates are provably impossible. Provides lower bound estimation algorithms. Evaluated on sepsis management.

papers.label: Predicting the Need for Blood Transfusion in Intensive Care Units with Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Yuqing Wang, Yun Zhao, Linda Petzold
papers.detail: Uses discretized BCQ for transfusion decision-making. Evaluates state representation and reward design. Transfer learning improves performance on data-scarce datasets by up to 17%.

papers.label: Horizon Reduction as Information Loss in Offline Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Uday Kumar Nidadala, Venkata Bhumika Guthi
papers.detail: Proves horizon reduction in offline RL can induce fundamental information loss. Identifies three structural failure modes: prefix indistinguishability, objective misspecification, and representation aliasing. Optimal policies may be indistinguishable from suboptimal ones even with infinite data.

papers.label: Optimizing Warfarin Dosing Using Contextual Bandit: An Offline Policy Learning and Evaluation Method
papers.authors: Yong Huang, Charles A. Downs, Amir M. Rahmani
papers.detail: Uses offline policy learning in contextual bandit setting for warfarin dosing. Learned policies surpass baselines without genotype inputs, even with suboptimal demonstrations.

papers.label: Flexible Blood Glucose Control: Offline Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback
papers.authors: Harry Emerson, Sam Gordon James, Matthew Guy, Ryan McConville
papers.detail: PAINT framework: offline RL from human feedback for insulin dosing. Uses sketch-based reward learning from patient records. Reduces glycaemic risk by 15% over commercial benchmark.

papers.label: Precision Physical Activity Prescription via Reinforcement Learning for Functional Actions
papers.authors: Gefei Lin, Rui Miao, Jennifer Sacheck, Xiaoke Zhang
papers.detail: Develops offline RL algorithm for personalized daily step distribution. Uses All of Us Research Program data. Learned policy suggests more steps and consistent patterns, with subgroup-specific recommendations.

papers.label: Guardian-regularized Safe Offline Reinforcement Learning for Smart Weaning of Mechanical Circulatory Devices
papers.authors: Aysin Tumay, Sophia Sun, Sonia Fereidooni, Aaron Dumas, Elise Jortberg, Rose Yu
papers.detail: Clinically-aware OOD-regularized Model-based Policy Optimization (CORMPO) for weaning MCS devices. Transformer-based digital twin for evaluation. 28% higher reward than baselines and 82.6% higher in clinical metrics.

coverage_notes: Covers CQL, BCQ, IQL, AWR, Decision Transformer, SPIBB, conformal RL, model-based offline RL, and safe RL methods applied to sepsis, diabetes, mechanical ventilation, anesthesia, blood transfusion, insulin management, population health, and mobile health. Includes negative results (Luo et al. 2024), failure mode analysis (Nidadala & Guthi 2025), and confounding challenges (Pace et al. 2023, Kausik et al. 2022).
